title: Serialize env-mutating config/secrets/doctor tests behind a shared mutex
action: Root cause (confirmed by reading the code): std::env::set_var/remove_var mutate process-wide state, but cargo test runs #[test] fns in parallel threads, so tests that mutate HERDR_GO_GITHUB_TOKEN, HERDR_GO_WEB_SECRET, HERDR_GO_TELEGRAM_TOKEN, or XDG_DATA_HOME race each other across THREE files: src/config/mod.rs, src/config/secrets.rs, src/doctor/checks.rs. Fix: add `#[cfg(test)] pub(crate) static ENV_TEST_LOCK: std::sync::Mutex<()> = std::sync::Mutex::new(());` at module scope in src/config/mod.rs, placed just before `mod tests` (visible crate-wide as crate::config::ENV_TEST_LOCK). In config/mod.rs's own tests it is in scope via the existing `use super::*;`. In every test below, acquire it as the FIRST statement and keep the returned guard alive for the test's whole body (bind to `_env_guard`, poison-tolerant via `.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())`): config/mod.rs::secrets_absent_from_env_and_file_are_none, config/mod.rs::data_dir_defaults_to_home_local_share, config/mod.rs::ensure_web_secret_prefers_env, config/secrets.rs::absent_env_file_yields_all_none_and_no_error (use crate::config::ENV_TEST_LOCK), config/secrets.rs::env_file_provides_fallback_when_process_env_absent (use crate::config::ENV_TEST_LOCK), config/secrets.rs::process_env_wins_over_env_file (use crate::config::ENV_TEST_LOCK), config/secrets.rs::env_file_failing_protection_is_ignored_not_used (use crate::config::ENV_TEST_LOCK), doctor/checks.rs::web_token_readonly_prefers_env (use crate::config::ENV_TEST_LOCK). Do not change any assertion or behavior -- only add synchronization. Do not touch tests that never call std::env::set_var/remove_var (e.g. macos_config_and_data_dir_use_application_support only reads HOME, no writer contends it).

truths: ENV_TEST_LOCK is defined exactly once (src/config/mod.rs) and reused by secrets.rs and doctor/checks.rs via crate::config::ENV_TEST_LOCK -- never duplicated per file
All 8 named tests acquire the lock as their first statement and hold the guard for their full body
Lock acquisition is poison-tolerant (a prior panicking guarded test must not poison the mutex for later tests)
No test's assertions or behavior changed -- only synchronization added
Running the full cargo test suite 30 times in a row (no --test-threads=1 override) never fails

artifacts.substantive: ENV_TEST_LOCK static added; its 3 env-mutating tests hold the guard

prohibitions: Do not use a scoped-env crate or unsafe env manipulation -- mutex serialization only, per the accepted backlog direction
Do not add --test-threads=1 to the crate's default test invocation -- that would slow every future test run project-wide instead of fixing the specific race
Do not weaken or delete the assertions in any of the 8 tests

verify: for i in $(seq 1 30); do cargo test --quiet || { echo FAILED_AT_ITERATION_$i; exit 1; }; done && cargo fmt --all --check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && echo ALL_GREEN

trace.outcome: Added a shared #[cfg(test)] ENV_TEST_LOCK mutex in src/config/mod.rs and acquired it as the first statement (poison-tolerant) in the 8 named env-mutating tests across mod.rs, secrets.rs, and doctor/checks.rs to serialize process-env mutation and eliminate cross-test races under the default parallel test runner. secrets.rs was edited by the orchestrator directly (user-approved out-of-band due to a privacy-guard false positive on the filename); mod.rs and doctor/checks.rs were edited by this worker (Kevin). Verified: 30x cargo test loop all green (273 tests each), cargo fmt --all --check clean, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.
trace.verify_output: 30/30 iterations: 273 tests passed, 0 failed each run; cargo fmt --all --check clean; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; final line: ALL_GREEN
trace.verify_command: for i in $(seq 1 30); do cargo test --quiet || { echo FAILED_AT_ITERATION_$i; exit 1; }; done && cargo fmt --all --check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && echo ALL_GREEN
